design_philosophy.core_loop: Match cookies of the same type in rows or columns by rotating grid lines, clear completed lines, manage incoming cookies from top/right edges until grid overflow
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure vs. spatial reasoning: players must rapidly identify matching patterns while managing the growing grid and preventing overflow
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Puzzle mastery through elegant matching mechanics; competitive social play in VS mode where players can disrupt opponents
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rubik's Cube-style rotation mechanic for grid manipulation (unusual for tile-matching games of the era); wild-card Yoshi head cookie breaks pattern rigidity; strong emphasis on competitive asymmetric gameplay where one player's clears can harm the opponent

difficulty.continues_system: Grid overflow triggers game over; no explicit continue/lives system documented
difficulty.checkpointing: Progressive level-based progression in Action Mode; Puzzle Mode offers discrete challenge levels with move limits
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Action Mode increases complexity across successive levels; Puzzle Mode offers preset challenge scenarios with predefined grid states and move constraints; VS Mode difficulty scales with opponent (computer AI in SNES single-player VS mode)

progression.unlock_mechanism: Action Mode requires completing successive levels to advance; Puzzle Mode levels are independently selectable discrete challenges
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer VS Mode with split-screen competitive play
Puzzle Mode with predefined grids and move-limit constraints encouraging optimization
Computer opponent in SNES single-player VS Mode
Multiple game modes (Action, VS, Puzzle) offer different progression paths

world_building_techniques: Thematic cosmetic framing: Yoshi/Mario character presence and cookie jar aesthetic
End-of-round cutscenes (documented as existing in Round 6 with Mario and plane foreground elements)
Character sprites appear in VS Mode (Yoshi, Mario, Bowser, Peach unused/present in graphics)
Cookie jar skit motif referenced across NES, Game Boy, and SNES versions

danger_signals: VS Mode mechanic explicitly allows players to disrupt opponents through their own match clears
Competitive split-screen design centers on direct player conflict
environmental_storytelling: Cookie jar setting provides thematic context for tile-matching objective
Mario universe character integration (Yoshi, Mario, Bowser, Peach) grounds puzzle mechanics in familiar IP

sources_used: tcrf: Yoshi's Cookie (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
